root_text.an6.45:2.1: “Yampi, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko iṇaṁ ādiyati, iṇādānampi, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino”ti?
root_text.an6.45:3.1: “Yampi, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko iṇaṁ ādiyitvā vaḍḍhiṁ paṭissuṇāti, vaḍḍhipi, bhikkhave, dukkhā lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino”ti?
root_text.an6.45:4.1: “Yampi, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko vaḍḍhiṁ paṭissuṇitvā kālābhataṁ vaḍḍhiṁ na deti, codentipi naṁ;
root_text.an6.45:5.1: “Yampi, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko codiyamāno na deti, anucarantipi naṁ;
root_text.an6.45:6.1: “Yampi, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko anucariyamāno na deti, bandhantipi naṁ;
root_text.an6.45:7.1: “Iti kho, bhikkhave, dāliddiyampi dukkhaṁ lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino, iṇādānampi dukkhaṁ lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino, vaḍḍhipi dukkhā lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino, codanāpi dukkhā lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino, anucariyāpi dukkhā lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino, bandhanampi dukkhaṁ lokasmiṁ kāmabhogino;
root_text.an6.45:7.2: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci saddhā natthi kusalesu dhammesu, hirī natthi kusalesu dhammesu, ottappaṁ natthi kusalesu dhammesu, vīriyaṁ natthi kusalesu dhammesu, paññā natthi kusalesu dhammesu—
root_text.an6.45:7.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye daliddo assako anāḷhiko.
root_text.an6.45:8.1: Sa kho so, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko saddhāya asati kusalesu dhammesu, hiriyā asati kusalesu dhammesu, ottappe asati kusalesu dhammesu, vīriye asati kusalesu dhammesu, paññāya asati kusalesu dhammesu, kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an6.45:9.1: So tassa kāyaduccaritassa paṭicchādanahetu pāpikaṁ icchaṁ paṇidahati.
root_text.an6.45:9.2: ‘Mā maṁ jaññū’ti icchati, ‘mā maṁ jaññū’ti saṅkappati, ‘mā maṁ jaññū’ti vācaṁ bhāsati, ‘mā maṁ jaññū’ti kāyena parakkamati. So tassa vacīduccaritassa paṭicchādanahetu …pe… so tassa manoduccaritassa paṭicchādanahetu …pe… ‘mā maṁ jaññū’ti kāyena parakkamati.
root_text.an6.45:11.1: Tamenaṁ araññagataṁ vā rukkhamūlagataṁ vā suññāgāragataṁ vā vippaṭisārasahagatā pāpakā akusalavitakkā samudācaranti.
root_text.an6.45:12.1: Sa kho so, bhikkhave, daliddo assako anāḷhiko kāyena duccaritaṁ caritvā vācāya duccaritaṁ caritvā manasā duccaritaṁ caritvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā nirayabandhane vā bajjhati tiracchānayonibandhane vā.
root_text.an6.45:12.2: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekabandhanampi samanupassāmi evaṁdāruṇaṁ evaṅkaṭukaṁ evaṁantarāyakaraṁ anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya, yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, nirayabandhanaṁ vā tiracchānayonibandhanaṁ vāti.

translation_text.an6.45:1.1: “Mendicants, isn’t poverty suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:2.1: “When a poor, penniless person falls into debt, isn’t being in debt also suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:3.1: “When a poor person who has fallen into debt agrees to pay interest, isn’t the interest also suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:4.1: “When a poor person who has fallen into debt and agreed to pay interest fails to pay it when it falls due, they get a warning.
translation_text.an6.45:4.2: Isn’t being warned suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:5.1: “When a poor person fails to pay after getting a warning, they’re prosecuted.
translation_text.an6.45:5.2: Isn’t being prosecuted suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:6.1: “When a poor person fails to pay after being prosecuted, they’re imprisoned.
translation_text.an6.45:6.2: Isn’t being imprisoned suffering in the world for a person who enjoys sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.an6.45:7.1: “So mendicants, poverty, debt, interest, warnings, prosecution, and imprisonment are suffering in the world for those who enjoy sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an6.45:7.2: In the same way, whoever has no faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities
translation_text.an6.45:7.3: is called poor and penniless in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an6.45:8.1: Since they have no faith, conscience, prudence, energy, or wisdom when it comes to skillful qualities, they do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an6.45:8.2: This is how they’re in debt, I say.
translation_text.an6.45:9.1: In order to conceal the bad things they do by way of body, speech, and mind they harbor corrupt wishes.
translation_text.an6.45:9.2: They wish, plan, speak, and act with the thought: ‘May no-one find me out!’
translation_text.an6.45:9.3: This is how they pay interest, I say.
translation_text.an6.45:10.1: Good-hearted spiritual companions say this about them:
translation_text.an6.45:10.2: ‘This venerable acts like this, and behaves like that.’
translation_text.an6.45:10.3: This is how they’re warned, I say.
translation_text.an6.45:11.1: When they go to a wilderness, the root of a tree, or an empty hut, they’re beset by remorseful, unskillful thoughts.
translation_text.an6.45:11.2: This is how they’re prosecuted, I say.
translation_text.an6.45:12.1: That poor, penniless person has done bad things by way of body, speech, and mind. When their body breaks up, after death, they’re trapped in the prison of hell or the animal realm.
translation_text.an6.45:12.2: I don’t see a single prison that’s as grim, as vicious, and such an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke as the prison of hell or the animal realm.
translation_text.an6.45:13.1: Poverty is said to be suffering in the world,
translation_text.an6.45:13.2: and so is being in debt.
translation_text.an6.45:13.3: A poor person who has fallen into debt
translation_text.an6.45:13.4: frets even when spending the loan.
translation_text.an6.45:14.4: for someone who prays for pleasure and possessions.
translation_text.an6.45:15.1: In the same way, in the noble one’s training
translation_text.an6.45:16.4: they wish, ‘May no-one find me out!’
translation_text.an6.45:17.4: on and on, life after life.
translation_text.an6.45:18.3: is a poor person who has fallen into debt,
translation_text.an6.45:18.4: and frets even when spending the loan.
translation_text.an6.45:19.1: And when in village or wilderness
translation_text.an6.45:20.3: goes to one of the animal realms,
translation_text.an6.45:21.2: from which the attentive are released.
translation_text.an6.45:21.4: with wealth that is properly earned.
translation_text.an6.45:22.2: holds a winning hand on both counts:
translation_text.an6.45:22.3: welfare and benefit in this life,
translation_text.an6.45:22.5: This is how, for a householder,
translation_text.an6.45:23.1: In the same way, in the noble one’s training,
translation_text.an6.45:24.3: After gaining pleasure not of the flesh,
translation_text.an6.45:25.1: They give up the five hindrances,
translation_text.an6.45:26.2: regards the ending of all fetters,
translation_text.an6.45:26.3: by not grasping in any way,
translation_text.an6.45:27.1: To that unaffected one, rightly freed
translation_text.an6.45:27.2: with the end of the fetters of continued existence,
translation_text.an6.45:28.4: this is the highest freedom from debt.”

variant_text.an6.45:2.1: daliddo → daḷiddo (bj, sya-all) | anāḷhiko → anāḷhiyo (bj); anāḷiko (si, pts1ed); anaddhiko (sya-all, km)
